dataSources.title: External Data Source Connectivity
dataSources.subtitle: None of the 4 authoritative sources support CORS, so the backend (/admin/datasources) probes them and returns status, latency and sampled data.

rules.title: Scoring & Labelling Rules (Guideline-based)
rules.subtitle: These 6 authoritative guidelines are rule sources, not databases: what each one is, which NutriKids rule it becomes, and how to implement it.

models.title: Scoring Models
models.subtitle: Three complementary roles: Nutri-Score grades, NRF ranks, FDA Healthy badges — all alongside the 4-dimension personalised score.

recommendation.sourcesText: USDA FoodData Central, EFSA, PubMed, peer-reviewed studies, and verified manufacturer data.

landing.subtitle: Help us build a healthier future for children.
landing.desc: NutriKids is a nonprofit, community-driven initiative. Whether through financial support, technical contributions, research collaboration, or sharing our mission, you can help us make nutrition guidance more accessible for families.

form.title: Become a Contributor
form.subtitle: Help us improve children's nutrition education and wellbeing.
form.desc1: NutriKids welcomes individuals and organizations who would like to contribute their time, expertise, or resources to support healthier childhood development.
form.desc2: We are particularly interested in collaborating with:
form.desc3: Whether you can contribute technical expertise, research support, educational content, design skills, or community outreach, we would love to hear from you.
form.note: Please note: Most contributor opportunities are currently volunteer-based. As NutriKids continues to grow, some roles may become eligible for paid support depending on available funding, project needs, work authorization requirements, and role availability. Compensation is not guaranteed, and participation should be considered voluntary unless otherwise agreed upon in writing.

questions.q2: What is the best way to contact you?
questions.q2Hint: Examples: Email, LinkedIn, phone number, personal website, or other preferred contact method.
questions.q3: Which area(s) would you like to contribute to?
questions.q4: What skills, experience, or expertise would you like to contribute?
questions.q4Hint: Examples: Programming, nutrition research, public health, education, design, data analysis, community engagement, fundraising, communications, etc.
questions.q5: Approximately how much time could you contribute?
questions.q6: Have you already sent your resume and/or cover letter to
questions.q7: Why are you interested in contributing to NutriKids?
questions.q7HintFull: We would love to learn more about your interests, goals, or motivation for getting involved.
questions.q8: Is there anything else you would like us to know?

success.title: Thank You!
messages: Thank you for your interest in supporting NutriKids.
Our team reviews submissions periodically and may contact contributors when opportunities align with project needs and available resources.
We appreciate your willingness to help improve children's nutrition education and wellbeing.

resumeOptions.planned: Not yet, but I plan to

panelAbout.title: About NutriKids
paragraphs: NutriKids is an evidence-informed nutrition education platform designed to help families understand how food choices may support their children's unique growth and developmental needs.

By combining nutrition science, publicly available food and nutrition data, and artificial intelligence technology, NutriKids translates complex food information into practical, child-centered insights that are easier for families to understand and apply.
Unlike general food analysis tools, NutriKids is being developed to help parents evaluate foods in the context of a child's age, growth stage, and nutritional needs.

Our first release focuses on the Food Analyzer, which helps families better understand packaged foods and how their nutritional characteristics may support different aspects of child development. Future releases will introduce Daily Nutrition Tracking and Personalized Nutrition Plans to provide a more comprehensive and individualized nutrition experience.
Our long-term vision is to create a trusted nutrition companion that helps families make informed decisions and build healthier habits throughout childhood.

panelMission.title: Mission
paragraphs: At NutriKids, we believe that every child deserves access to nutrition guidance that reflects their unique developmental needs.
Our mission is to empower parents and caregivers with accessible, trustworthy, and science-informed nutrition insights that support healthier food choices and lifelong wellbeing.
By making nutrition information easier to understand and apply, we aim to help families build healthy habits and make confident decisions about the foods their children consume.

foodAnalyzer.title: Food Analyzer
foodAnalyzer.desc: The Food Analyzer evaluates packaged foods based on ingredients, nutrient content, and food characteristics. Rather than simply displaying nutrition facts, NutriKids helps parents understand how nutrients and ingredients may contribute to different aspects of child growth and development while highlighting nutritional strengths and potential areas of concern.

dailyTracking.title: Daily Nutrition Tracking
dailyTracking.desc: Daily Nutrition Tracking will help families evaluate a child's overall nutrient intake throughout the day and better understand dietary patterns over time.

personalizedPlans.title: Personalized Nutrition Plans
personalizedPlans.desc: Personalized Nutrition Plans will provide tailored nutrition guidance based on factors such as age, growth stage, dietary habits, and individual nutritional needs.

approach.title: Our Approach
approach.desc: NutriKids is designed to support—not replace—parental judgment and professional healthcare guidance. Our goal is to make nutrition information easier to understand so families can make informed decisions with greater confidence.

panelTeam.title: Team
panelTeam.desc: NutriKids is developed by a multidisciplinary team with backgrounds in research, product design, data science, and software development. The project is led by SENSE Institute, a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing community wellbeing through research, education, and innovation.
panelTeam.footer: We welcome collaboration with healthcare professionals, nutrition experts, educators, researchers, technologists, and community partners who share our mission.

panelSources.title: Scientific Sources
panelSources.desc: NutriKids is informed by publicly available scientific evidence, nutrition research, and guidance from recognized health and nutrition organizations, including:

items.label: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
Peer-reviewed scientific literature and publicly available nutrition databases

panelSources.footer: We regularly review available resources and update our educational content to reflect current evidence and best practices whenever possible.

scoreCalc.title: How the Score Is Calculated
scoreCalc.desc: NutriKids' food scores are computed in two steps by a deterministic rule engine, not randomly generated by AI, so results stay stable, auditable, and traceable:

dims.label: Nutrient Balance (NutriNorm)
dims.desc: Based on the Nutri-Score system: energy, added sugar, saturated fat, and sodium count as negative points (max 55); fruit/veg content, fiber, and protein count as positive points (max 17), combined into a 0-1 balance score

dims.label: Development Match (DevScore)
dims.desc: Compares the food's nutrients against the child's age-based Daily Reference Intake (DRI), weighted by developmental goal priority (core/important/supporting), producing a 0-1 match score

dims.label: Final Score
dims.desc: Final Score = 100 × Nutrient Balance × (0.3 + 0.7 × Development Match). The more balanced the nutrition and the better it fits the child's current developmental needs, the higher the score

scoreCalc.note: Score formulas are refined as more research becomes available. AI is only used to translate the calculated result into a plain-language explanation — it does not participate in the core scoring.

panelAIDisclaimer.title: AI Disclaimer
panelAIDisclaimer.p1: NutriKids uses artificial intelligence (AI) technology to help analyze food and nutrition information and generate educational insights.
panelAIDisclaimer.pillText: While we strive to provide accurate and useful information, AI-generated content may occasionally contain inaccuracies, omissions, or interpretations that do not fully reflect an individual's unique circumstances.
panelAIDisclaimer.p2: Information provided by NutriKids is intended for educational purposes only and should not be considered medical, nutritional, or professional advice.

panelMedicalDisclaimer.title: Medical Disclaimer
panelMedicalDisclaimer.p1: NutriKids is an educational platform and does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, treatment, or healthcare services.
panelMedicalDisclaimer.pillText: Information provided through NutriKids is intended for educational and informational purposes only and should not replace professional medical or nutritional guidance.
panelMedicalDisclaimer.p2: Parents and caregivers should consult qualified healthcare professionals regarding questions about a child's health, nutrition, growth, or development.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: NutriKids is committed to protecting your privacy and handling your information responsibly. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and the choices available to you when using NutriKids.

sections.title: Information We May Collect
sections.content: Depending on how you use NutriKids, we may collect:
items: Information you voluntarily provide, such as age group preferences, nutrition-related inputs, or feedback submissions
Food and nutrition information submitted for analysis
Usage and technical information, including browser type, device information, and website analytics
Contact information provided through support requests or donations

sections.title: How We Use Information
sections.content: We may use collected information to:
items: Generate nutrition analyses and educational insights
Understand how users interact with the platform
Improve functionality, accuracy, and user experience
Respond to inquiries and support requests
Support research, evaluation, and product development

sections.title: Data Sharing
sections.content: NutriKids does not sell personal information. We may share information with trusted service providers that help operate the platform, such as hosting, analytics, payment processing, or AI service providers, when necessary to deliver services.

sections.title: Children's Privacy
sections.content: NutriKids is designed to support families and caregivers. Parents and caregivers should provide information on behalf of children. We do not knowingly collect personal information directly from children without appropriate parental involvement.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: We take reasonable measures to protect information from unauthorized access, loss, misuse, or disclosure. However, no online service can guarantee absolute security.

sections.title: Updates to This Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. Updated versions will be posted on this page with a revised effective date.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have questions regarding this Privacy Policy, please contact us through the contact information provided on the website.

sections.title: Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing or using NutriKids, you agree to these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with these terms, please discontinue use of the platform.

sections.title: Educational Purpose
sections.content: NutriKids is an educational platform designed to help families better understand food, nutrition, and healthy development. The platform is intended for informational and educational purposes only.

sections.title: No Medical Advice
sections.content: NutriKids does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, treatment, or healthcare services. Information provided through the platform should not be considered a substitute for professional medical, nutritional, or healthcare guidance.

sections.title: User Responsibility
sections.content: Users are responsible for evaluating information provided by NutriKids and determining its suitability for their individual circumstances. Parents and caregivers should consult qualified healthcare professionals regarding questions about a child's health, nutrition, growth, or development.

sections.title: Accuracy of Information
sections.content: We strive to provide accurate and evidence-informed information. However, NutriKids does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, reliability, or suitability of any content, analysis, or recommendation. Information may change as scientific knowledge evolves.

sections.title: Intellectual Property
sections.content: Unless otherwise stated, content, design, logos, text, and materials available through NutriKids are the property of NutriKids, SENSE Institute, or their respective owners and may not be reproduced without permission.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
sections.content: To the fullest extent permitted by law, NutriKids, SENSE Institute, and project contributors shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, or special damages arising from the use of, or inability to use, the platform.

sections.title: Modifications
sections.content: We may modify, suspend, or discontinue any portion of NutriKids at any time without prior notice.

sections.title: Updates to These Terms
sections.content: We may update these Terms of Use periodically. Continued use of NutriKids after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have questions regarding these Terms of Use, please contact us through the contact information provided on the website.
